summary: fabric-cli and fabric-server are SEPARATE workspace packages linked via `workspace:*` dependency. CLI imports server functions directly at runtime (not bundled). `fab serve` uses HTTP/StreamableHTTP transport (not stdio). Client config writers for all 7 clients exist but the command string installed in MCP configs points to the server package's dist/index.js path resolved at runtime via import.meta.resolve — making a global-only `npm install -g @fenglimg/fabric-cli` INSUFFICIENT unless fabric-server is also globally linked.

integration_points.description: fab serve imports startHttpServer directly from @fenglimg/fabric-server package. The dependency is `workspace:*` in packages/cli/package.json (line 21). At runtime, Node resolves @fenglimg/fabric-server from node_modules next to the CLI installation. The server is NOT bundled into the CLI dist — tsup.config has no external: false or bundle flags covering the server.
integration_points.implication_for_global_install: When `npm install -g @fenglimg/fabric-cli`, npm will also install @fenglimg/fabric-server into the global node_modules (because it is a production dependency). This part works.

integration_points.boundary: CLI → fabric-server (config install — server path resolution)
integration_points.description: fab config install computes the server path via `import.meta.resolve('@fenglimg/fabric-server')`. This resolves the PHYSICAL PATH to fabric-server/dist/index.js and writes it into the client's MCP config file as the `args[0]` of the Node.js server entry. The serverEntry written looks like: {command: process.execPath, args: ['/path/to/node_modules/@fenglimg/fabric-server/dist/index.js']}.
integration_points.implication_for_global_install: If run globally, the path written will be something like /usr/local/lib/node_modules/@fenglimg/fabric-cli/node_modules/@fenglimg/fabric-server/dist/index.js (or global prefix). This is fine for global mode. If run locally (project node_modules .bin/fab), the path will be project-local. FAB_SERVER_PATH env override allows bypassing auto-resolution.

integration_points.description: fab serve starts an HTTP server (Express + StreamableHTTPServerTransport from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk) on port 7373. However, fab config install writes STDIO-mode server entries into client configs — the entry is {command: node, args: [serverPath]} which causes the MCP client to launch the server as a child process over stdio. There are TWO transport modes: (1) fab serve = HTTP/SSE/StreamableHTTP for dashboard + web clients; (2) client configs installed by fab config install = stdio child process invocations. These are separate use cases.
integration_points.stdio_entry_point: packages/server/src/index.ts line 83-87 — when run as main module (node server/dist/index.js), falls through to startStdioServer()
integration_points.http_entry_point: packages/server/src/index.ts line 55 — startHttpServer() called by CLI serve command

integration_points.boundary: fab init → .claude/ artifacts (Claude-specific, not MCP config)
integration_points.description: fab init creates project-scoped .claude/settings.json (merges Stop hook), .claude/hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs (executable), and .claude/skills/agents-md-init/SKILL.md. Critically, fab init does NOT call fab config install — it only sets up the Claude hook/skill scaffold. The MCP server is NOT configured by fab init; that requires a separate call to fab config install.
integration_points.gap: fab init does not auto-configure MCP clients, leaving users to run fab config install separately

integration_points.description: ClaudeCodeCLIWriter writes to {workspaceRoot}/.claude/settings.json under mcpServers.fabric. Detection: checks for ~/.claude OR {workspaceRoot}/.claude existence. Default config path is always PROJECT-SCOPED (.claude/settings.json in workspaceRoot), never global ~/.claude/settings.json. This scopes MCP to the project, which is intentional.

ClaudeCodeDesktopWriter writes to platform-specific global Claude Desktop config (~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json on macOS). Uses same JSON mcpServers schema. This is GLOBAL scope — applies to all projects opened in Claude Desktop.
CursorWriter detects .cursor directory in workspaceRoot and writes to {workspaceRoot}/.cursor/mcp.json. Project-scoped.
WindsurfWriter detects .windsurf directory in workspaceRoot and writes to {workspaceRoot}/.windsurf/mcp.json. Project-scoped.

GeminiCLIWriter detects ~/.gemini OR GEMINI.md in workspaceRoot. Writes to ~/.gemini/settings.json — this is USER-GLOBAL scope, not project-scoped. All projects using Gemini CLI share one server entry.
CodexTOMLConfigWriter detects ~/.codex directory and writes to ~/.codex/config.toml under [mcp.servers.fabric]. This is USER-GLOBAL scope. Schema differs: TOML format with mcp.servers.fabric table.

GeminiCLI.detection_signal: ~/.gemini directory exists OR GEMINI.md in workspaceRoot

transport_model.fab_serve_transport: HTTP/StreamableHTTP (Streamable HTTP from MCP SDK) on port 7373. Express app with /mcp endpoint. Used for dashboard UI and web-based MCP sessions.
transport_model.client_config_transport: stdio — all 7 client writers create {command: process.execPath, args: [serverPath]} entries. MCP client (Cursor, Claude, etc.) spawns the server as a Node child process reading stdin/stdout. The server auto-detects main module invocation and calls startStdioServer().
transport_model.server_main_detection: packages/server/src/index.ts lines 79-88 — if process.argv[1] resolves to the module file, startStdioServer() is called automatically. No explicit mode flag needed.

global_vs_local_install.q4_answer: CONDITIONALLY SUFFICIENT. `npm install -g @fenglimg/fabric-cli` IS sufficient IF the user only uses `fab config install` to wire client configs. fabric-server is a production dep of fabric-cli and will be installed alongside it in global node_modules. The server path written into client configs will be the global path, which is persistent and correct.
global_vs_local_install.q4_caveat: INSUFFICIENT for LOCAL/PROJECT mode: If users want a project-local server version (e.g., workspace package, different version per project), global install is not enough. The path baked into client configs will be the global path — if the user updates or removes the global install, client configs break.
global_mode_pros: Server path in configs is stable
npm/pnpm handles server install as transitive dep
global_mode_cons: All projects share the same server version
If global is uninstalled/updated, all client configs silently break (stale path)
Path in MCP config is absolute and tied to global prefix (e.g., nvm switches can invalidate node path)
local_mode_pros: Path is relative to project node_modules
local_mode_cons: `fab config install` must be run from project context to write correct local path
Client configs in global-scope (Gemini, Codex) will have last-run project path — conflicts with other projects
global_vs_local_install.current_design: Currently there is NO local vs global mode switch. The code always uses import.meta.resolve('@fenglimg/fabric-server') at runtime — which resolves relative to where the fab binary is. Global install = global path. Local .bin/fab = local path.
global_vs_local_install.local_detection_gap: No mechanism to detect 'run from project-local node_modules/.bin/fab' vs 'run from global'. FAB_SERVER_PATH env is the only escape hatch.

project_scoped.purpose: Fabric Bootstrap Protocol markdown for AI agents
Claude Stop hook for agents-md-init-reminder — NOT MCP config
Hook script run on every Claude Stop event
Claude skill for init reminder workflow
Written by `fab config install` — NOTE: gemini is global-user scope, not project

init_artifact_catalog.important_separation: fab init does NOT call fab config install. MCP client wiring is a SEPARATE step. init only creates: AGENTS.md + .fabric/ + Claude hook/skill scaffold.

gaps_and_clarification_needs.question: Q7: Local vs global MCP server install mode — no current mechanism
gaps_and_clarification_needs.detail: The codebase has no concept of 'local project server' vs 'global server'. import.meta.resolve always resolves from where fab runs. To support local mode: either (a) detect if @fenglimg/fabric-server exists in workspaceRoot node_modules and prefer that path, or (b) add a --mode local|global flag to `fab config install`.

gaps_and_clarification_needs.question: Q5/Q6: fab init should optionally auto-call fab config install
gaps_and_clarification_needs.detail: Currently init does not configure MCP clients at all (only Claude hooks). For a smoother onboarding, init could offer to call config install for detected clients. This would require resolver.ts to run within init, and the user would need @fenglimg/fabric-server resolvable.

gaps_and_clarification_needs.question: Claude client config schema — what does claude-code CLI read for MCP?
gaps_and_clarification_needs.detail: ClaudeCodeCLIWriter writes to {workspaceRoot}/.claude/settings.json with mcpServers key. However, fab init also writes to .claude/settings.json with a hooks key — these coexist in the same file. The schema is {hooks: {...}, mcpServers: {fabric: {...}}}. The merge logic in init.ts (mergeClaudeStopHook) does not touch mcpServers, so config install can write to the same file without conflict.
gaps_and_clarification_needs.status: RESOLVED — no conflict, both keys coexist

gaps_and_clarification_needs.question: Server path staleness: global node upgrade or nvm switch breaks all configs
gaps_and_clarification_needs.detail: The command in client MCP configs uses process.execPath (absolute node binary path) at config-write time. If user switches node versions via nvm, process.execPath changes but the baked path in client configs remains stale. Consider using 'node' (from PATH) instead of absolute path, or document this limitation.

gaps_and_clarification_needs.question: fab serve (HTTP) is not wired into any client config — it's standalone only
gaps_and_clarification_needs.detail: There is no writer or command that configures an MCP client to connect to the HTTP server. All client configs use stdio subprocess mode. If a client supports HTTP MCP transport, there is currently no way to point it at `fab serve`'s endpoint via fab config install.
gaps_and_clarification_needs.status: BY DESIGN for now — stdio is more universally supported

key_files.rationale: Declares @fenglimg/fabric-server as workspace:* production dependency — confirms server is a runtime dep (not bundled) that ships alongside CLI in npm installs
Entry point for `fab serve` — directly imports and calls startHttpServer() from @fenglimg/fabric-server. Defines the HTTP transport coupling between CLI and server.

key_code.description: Imports and invokes HTTP server from @fenglimg/fabric-server with port/projectRoot/host/authToken options
Port 7373 — the HTTP MCP server default port

key_files.topic_relation: Primary CLI→server coupling point for HTTP mode. Establishes that fab serve spawns HTTP transport, not stdio.

key_files.rationale: Implements `fab config install` — resolves the fabric-server absolute path via import.meta.resolve and writes it into all detected MCP client configs. Critical for Q4 (global install sufficiency).

key_code.description: Resolves absolute path to @fenglimg/fabric-server/dist/index.js using import.meta.resolve, with FAB_SERVER_PATH env override. This path is baked into client MCP configs.
Detects clients via resolveClients(), computes serverPath, and calls writer.write(serverPath, workspaceRoot) for each detected client

key_files.topic_relation: Answers Q4: server path resolution determines whether global-only install is sufficient. Shows global install does work but has nvm/path staleness risk.

key_files.rationale: resolveClients() — detects which AI clients are installed and instantiates their config writers. The detection heuristics (directory existence checks) determine which clients get configured.

key_code.description: Checks ~/.claude, workspaceRoot/.claude, .cursor, .windsurf, .roo, ~/.gemini, GEMINI.md, ~/.codex to detect installed clients and return appropriate writers
Helper that adds writer to list if configuredPath is set OR if detection heuristic returns true

key_files.topic_relation: Defines the complete client detection boundary — which clients are auto-discovered and which require explicit fabric.config.json override.

key_files.rationale: Defines ClientKind, ClientConfigWriter interface, and createServerEntry() — the shared schema for all 7 client writers and the server entry format written into configs.

key_code.description: Creates {command: process.execPath, args: [serverPath]} — uses absolute node binary path at write time. Risk: stale path after nvm switch.
Union type: ClaudeCodeCLI | ClaudeCodeDesktop | Cursor | Windsurf | RooCode | GeminiCLI | CodexCLI — the complete list of supported clients

key_files.topic_relation: The server entry schema written into client configs — explains the command string MCP clients use to spawn the server.

key_files.rationale: Contains ClaudeCodeCLIWriter, CursorWriter, WindsurfWriter, RooCodeWriter, GeminiCLIWriter — 5 of 7 client writers implementing JSON config format with mcpServers.fabric key.

key_code.description: Always writes to PROJECT-SCOPED {workspaceRoot}/.claude/settings.json, but detection checks for ~/.claude OR workspaceRoot/.claude. Intentional: MCP is project-scoped even if Claude is installed globally.
Always writes to GLOBAL ~/.gemini/settings.json — different scoping from Cursor/Claude CLI writers

key_files.topic_relation: Reveals the mixed scoping: some clients get project-local MCP config (Claude CLI, Cursor, Windsurf, Roo), others get global (Gemini, Codex, ClaudeDesktop).

key_files.rationale: ClaudeCodeDesktopWriter writes to global Claude Desktop config. Also exports writeClaudeCodeAll() which writes to both CLI and Desktop configs — utility used for bulk Claude setup.
CodexTOMLConfigWriter — only TOML-format writer, writes to ~/.codex/config.toml with mcp.servers.fabric table. Global-user scope.

Server entry point — exports both startStdioServer() and startHttpServer(). Contains main module detection that auto-starts stdio server when invoked as `node index.js` (the mode used by client MCP configs).

key_code.description: Creates McpServer + StdioServerTransport, connects them. Used when server is spawned as MCP child process by AI clients.
Starts Express HTTP server with StreamableHTTP MCP transport on specified port. Used by `fab serve` command.
Auto-detects if running as main script and calls startStdioServer() — enables `node dist/index.js` to work as MCP stdio server without additional flags

key_files.topic_relation: Explains the dual transport model: HTTP for fab serve, stdio for client configs. The isMainModule guard is what makes client configs work with a simple node invocation.

key_files.rationale: fab init does NOT configure MCP — it only creates AGENTS.md, .fabric/, and Claude hook/skill scaffold. This is the gap where MCP client setup is missing from the init flow.

key_code.description: Creates AGENTS.md, .fabric/agents.meta.json, .fabric/human-lock.json, .fabric/forensic.json, .claude/settings.json (hooks only), .claude/hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs, .claude/skills/agents-md-init/SKILL.md. No MCP config writing.
Writes Claude Stop hook to .claude/settings.json — touches hooks key only, mcpServers key untouched, so fab config install can coexist in the same file

key_files.topic_relation: Answers Q5/Q6 gap: init sets up Claude hooks but does not auto-call config install. To support init-triggered MCP setup, this function would need to call resolveClients() and writers.

key_files.rationale: fab bootstrap install writes client-specific markdown prompt files (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, .cursor/rules/*.mdc etc.) — separate from MCP config install. Handles prompt bootstrap, not server wiring.

key_code.description: Maps bootstrap clients to target files: claude→CLAUDE.md, cursor→.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc, gemini→GEMINI.md, codex→AGENTS.md (header prepend)

key_files.topic_relation: Clarifies the two-step client integration: MCP server wiring (fab config install) + prompt bootstrap (fab bootstrap install) are separate commands that both need to run.

patterns.name: workspace-dep-not-bundled
patterns.description: fabric-server is a workspace:* prod dep but NOT bundled into CLI dist (tsup config has no bundle:true for it). At npm install time npm resolves and installs it alongside CLI. This means global `npm install -g @fenglimg/fabric-cli` automatically installs the server too.

patterns.name: import.meta.resolve-path-baking
patterns.description: Config writers bake the absolute server path at config-write time using import.meta.resolve(). This is fragile to nvm/node version changes and monorepo moves, but is the simplest cross-platform approach.

patterns.name: mixed-scope-client-configs
patterns.description: Project-scoped configs: ClaudeCodeCLI (.claude/settings.json in project), Cursor (.cursor/mcp.json), Windsurf (.windsurf/mcp.json), RooCode (.roo/mcp.json). Global-user-scoped: GeminiCLI (~/.gemini/settings.json), CodexCLI (~/.codex/config.toml), ClaudeCodeDesktop (~/Library/...).

patterns.name: dual-transport-model
patterns.description: fab serve = HTTP/StreamableHTTP on port 7373 for dashboard UI. fab config install = stdio child process entries for AI clients. These are independent use cases sharing the same server package but different entry paths.

patterns.name: init-config-separation
patterns.description: fab init and fab config install are deliberately separate commands. init creates AGENTS.md + .fabric + Claude hooks. config install wires MCP. There is no auto-wiring in init today.

constraints: fabric-server must be resolvable from where fab binary runs — either globally (global install) or locally (project node_modules). No bundling.
Client configs store absolute paths — changing node versions or installation prefix breaks configs silently.
Global-scope configs (Gemini, Codex) get the last-written server path — multi-project workspaces will overwrite each other.
fab init is non-destructive and idempotent-on-abort — it throws if .fabric/ or AGENTS.md already exists.
